extraction.one_line_summary: THOMAS Geraint won the 14km Stage 1 time trial by 5 seconds, gaining 12 seconds on Tony Martin in the final 5km through superior pacing rather than cornering aggression.
tactical_patterns: Thomas gained 12 seconds in final 5km on Martin, revealing pacing superiority matters more than early splits in wet TTs.
Team Sky's tire choice/pressure optimization produced four riders in top eight, exposing equipment edge over peloton in wet conditions.
Froome finished 35 seconds ahead of Porte despite similar GC ambitions, sending deliberate message about BMC leadership hierarchy weakness.

rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: Fastest at intermediate check then accelerated further, indicating exceptional TT pacing discipline under wet conditions
rider_signals.MARTIN Tony: negative: Lost 12 seconds to Thomas in final 5km after leading at checkpoint, suggesting either pacing error or equipment disadvantage
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: Sixth place, 35 seconds faster than main rival Porte on non-specialist terrain, establishing unexpected time gap on Stage 1
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: negative: Lost 47 seconds total, 35 seconds to Froome alone, revealing cautious cornering strategy cost more time than anticipated
rider_signals.KUENG Stefan: positive: Second place for BMC at young age marks him as future TT threat, only rider outside Sky to challenge
